methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series E → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.4x) to $315M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Climate & Energy (found 0 same-stage, 2 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 21 same-sector peers exist in Climate & Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

data.narrative:
Sunfire is a Series E climate and energy technology company that has raised $315M+ to date. The valuation universe comprises 9 private peers (including TAE Technologies, Redwood Materials, Helion, Commonwealth Fusion Systems, and QuantumScape) and 1 public comparable (Linergia, ticker LNZA), drawn from a broader sector of 21 Climate & Energy firms.

A stage-progression method was applied using the $315M total raised as a valuation proxy. This method applies a typical Series E-to-next-stage multiplier of 1.4x, yielding an implied valuation of $441M. A peer-median approach was not viable; while 21 same-sector peers exist, insufficient clustering emerged within narrow filters (same-stage, adjacent-stage, similar-tier), falling below the three-datapoint minimum required for reliable benchmarking. Applying an all-sector median would introduce material noise.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $375M (low) to $507M (high), with a median of $441M. This single-method triangulation carries elevated uncertainty. The key limitation is the absence of tightly comparable private financings at identical stage and capital tier; reliance on stage-progression multipliers assumes Sunfire's risk and growth profile align with sector norms. Resolution of this range would benefit from narrower peer data or clarity on revenue trajectory and commercialization timeline.
